name: Absa Bank Kenya
description: Absa Bank Kenya, formerly Barclays Bank Kenya, rebranded in 2020 following Barclays Group's full exit from the African continent. It is one of Kenya's largest commercial banks, offering retail, corporate, and investment banking services. Absa Group of South Africa holds a majority stake and provides strategic and technology backing.

name: ALP Industrial REIT
description: ALP Industrial REIT is a real estate investment trust focused on industrial and logistics properties in Kenya. It distributes rental income from warehousing and light industrial facilities as regular dividends to unitholders. Industrial REITs are a relatively new asset class in Kenya, and ALP is among the first dedicated industrial REITs listed on the NSE.

name: Africa Mega Agricorp
description: Africa Mega Agricorp is an agricultural holding company listed on the NSE, focused on large-scale commercial crop farming in Kenya. It targets grain and oilseed production for domestic and export markets. The company is among the smaller capitalisation agricultural names on the exchange.

name: BAT Kenya
description: British American Tobacco (BAT) Kenya is a subsidiary of BAT plc, one of the world's largest tobacco companies. It manufactures and markets cigarette brands including Sportsman, Embassy, and Dunhill for the Kenyan and regional market. BAT Kenya is consistently one of the highest dividend-yielding stocks on the NSE, prized by income investors.

name: BK Group
description: BK Group is the holding company for Bank of Kigali, Rwanda's largest commercial bank. Cross-listed on both the NSE and the Rwanda Stock Exchange, it offers retail, corporate, and SME banking services across Rwanda. Its share price often serves as a proxy for Rwanda's economic growth and financial inclusion drive.

name: BOC Kenya
description: BOC Kenya is a manufacturer and distributor of industrial, medical, and specialty gases, and a subsidiary of Linde plc, the German global industrial gas giant. Its products serve hospitals, manufacturing plants, construction sites, and welding operations. BOC Kenya holds a near-monopoly position in the Kenyan industrial gas market.

name: Britam Holdings
description: Britam Holdings is a diversified financial services group offering life insurance, general insurance, asset management, real estate, and banking across East and Central Africa. It operates in Kenya, Uganda, Tanzania, Rwanda, South Sudan, Mozambique, and Malawi. Britam is one of the largest publicly listed financial services companies in the region.

name: Carbacid Investments
description: Carbacid Investments produces carbon dioxide (CO₂) gas from natural underground sources in Kenya for use in food and beverage carbonation. Its largest customer is East African Breweries, which uses the CO₂ to carbonate beer and soft drinks. The company holds a near-monopoly in locally-sourced CO₂ supply in East Africa, giving it pricing power.

name: Car and General (K) Ltd
description: Car and General (K) Ltd is a distributor of motor vehicles, motorcycles, agricultural machinery, and industrial equipment across Kenya and East Africa. It represents brands including Tata Motors and various agricultural equipment manufacturers. The company's revenues are closely tied to vehicle import volumes and the availability of consumer and commercial credit.

name: CIC Insurance Group
description: CIC Insurance Group is uniquely owned primarily by Kenya's cooperative movement, giving it strong roots in rural Kenya and among SACCO members. It offers general insurance, life insurance, and asset management services. Its cooperative ownership model drives a broad distribution network that extends well beyond urban centres.

name: Co-operative Bank
description: Co-operative Bank of Kenya is jointly owned by Kenya's vast cooperative movement and retail shareholders, making it one of the country's top-three banks by deposits. It is known for its strong rural branch network, SACCO banking relationships, and affordable banking products for cooperative members. The bank has also expanded into South Sudan and other regional markets.

name: Crown Paints Kenya
description: Crown Paints Kenya manufactures and markets decorative and industrial paints under the Crown, Crown Duracoat, and Sadolin brands. It is one of the leading paint manufacturers in East Africa, with distribution across Kenya, Uganda, Tanzania, Rwanda, and Ethiopia. The company's revenues move closely with construction activity and real estate development cycles.

name: Centum Investment Company PLC
description: Centum Investment is Kenya's largest publicly listed investment company, with a portfolio spanning private equity, real estate (anchored by Two Rivers Lifestyle Centre in Nairobi), financial services, and power infrastructure. It invests in high-growth, unlisted businesses across Sub-Saharan Africa before seeking exits through trade sales or public listings. Centum is valued by investors at a discount or premium to its stated net asset value.

name: Diamond Trust Bank
description: Diamond Trust Bank (DTB) is a mid-tier commercial bank with roots in the East African Asian business community. It operates in Kenya, Uganda, Tanzania, Burundi, and Rwanda, giving it one of the widest regional footprints of any NSE-listed bank. The Aga Khan Fund for Economic Development holds a significant strategic stake.

name: East African Breweries
description: East African Breweries Limited (EABL) is the dominant brewer in East Africa, producing Tusker, Guinness, Senator Keg, and other brands across Kenya, Uganda, and Tanzania. It is a subsidiary of Diageo plc, the global alcoholic beverages giant. EABL's revenues are widely watched as a proxy for consumer spending power and middle-class growth in the region.

name: Eaagads Ltd
description: Eaagads Ltd is one of Kenya's oldest coffee farming companies, with arabica coffee estates in Kiambu County on the slopes of the Aberdares. It sells processed coffee at the Nairobi Coffee Exchange and is exposed to global arabica price cycles. The company is among the smallest capitalisation stocks on the NSE.

name: Equity Group Holdings
description: Equity Group Holdings is Kenya's largest bank by customer accounts, having grown from a micro-finance institution in the 1980s into a pan-African financial services group. It now operates in Kenya, DRC, Uganda, Tanzania, Rwanda, South Sudan, and Ethiopia, serving over 20 million customers. Equity pioneered agency banking and mobile-driven financial inclusion across Sub-Saharan Africa.

name: Eveready East Africa
description: Eveready East Africa distributes Energizer-brand batteries and related consumer products in Kenya and the wider East African region. The company has faced sustained headwinds from cheaper imported Chinese batteries and the shift away from disposable battery devices. Its market position has weakened significantly over the past decade.

name: Family Bank
description: Family Bank is a Kenyan commercial bank that converted from a building society, with a focus on the SME sector, retail customers, and diaspora remittances. It operates a wide agent banking network that extends into peri-urban and rural Kenya. The bank is classified as a tier-two lender and has been growing its digital banking channels.

name: New Gold Issuer (RP) Ltd
description: New Gold Issuer (RP) Ltd is a gold-backed exchange traded product listed on the NSE that tracks the price of gold in Kenyan shillings. It provides investors with a straightforward exchange-traded route to gold price exposure without holding physical bullion. The product is issued by Absa Bank and settles in USD equivalent.

name: Home Afrika
description: Home Afrika is a real estate development and investment company focused on affordable and middle-income housing in Kenya. The company has faced persistent project delays and funding constraints since its NSE listing. Its share price performance has remained weak relative to its initial public offering price.

name: HF Group
description: HF Group (formerly Housing Finance Company of Kenya) is Kenya's only specialist mortgage finance institution, mobilising long-term savings and providing home loans. The group has diversified into general commercial banking through its HFC Bank subsidiary. Its business model is uniquely exposed to the Kenyan property market and long-term interest rate movements.

name: I&M Holdings
description: I&M Holdings is the parent company of I&M Bank, a tier-two Kenyan commercial bank with operations in Kenya, Rwanda, Tanzania, Uganda, and Mauritius. The bank traces its roots to a finance house established in the 1970s and is known for strong trade finance and corporate banking capabilities. Proparco and DEG are among its development finance institution shareholders.

name: Jubilee Holdings
description: Jubilee Holdings is East Africa's largest composite insurer, offering life, medical, and general insurance alongside asset management across Kenya, Uganda, Tanzania, Burundi, and Mauritius. The group has a long track record of profitability and consistent dividend payments, making it a favourite among income-seeking investors. Allianz SE of Germany is a strategic shareholder, bringing global reinsurance and technical expertise.

name: Kapchorua Tea Kenya PLC
description: Kapchorua Tea Kenya grows and processes black CTC tea on estates in the Rift Valley, selling production at the Mombasa tea auction, the world's largest. The company is a subsidiary of Williamson Tea Holdings, a UK-based agribusiness group. Its profitability tracks global black tea prices and seasonal rainfall patterns in Kenya's tea-growing zones.

name: KCB Group
description: KCB Group is Kenya's largest bank by total assets, with subsidiaries spanning Kenya, South Sudan, Uganda, Tanzania, Rwanda, Burundi, Ethiopia, and DRC. Originally founded as Kenya Commercial Bank, it has undergone significant privatisation while the Government of Kenya retains a meaningful stake. Its extensive East and Central African network makes it one of the continent's leading retail banks.

name: KenGen
description: Kenya Electricity Generating Company (KenGen) is the dominant power generator in Kenya, producing approximately 75% of the country's installed generation capacity. Its generation mix is heavily weighted towards geothermal energy in the Olkaria fields, making Kenya one of the world's most renewable electricity markets. KenGen is majority-owned by the Government of Kenya and also exports power to Uganda and Tanzania.

name: Kenya Re-Insurance Corporation
description: Kenya Re-Insurance Corporation (Kenya Re) is Kenya's only locally incorporated reinsurance company and the largest reinsurer in the COMESA region. It underwrites life and non-life reinsurance business from over 50 African countries, providing an important layer of financial protection across the continent's insurance industry. The Government of Kenya holds a majority stake.

name: Kenya Pipeline Company
description: Kenya Pipeline Company is the sole operator of the petroleum product pipeline system in Kenya, transporting refined fuel from the Mombasa port inland to storage terminals across the country. It also manages critical petroleum storage infrastructure that supports Kenya's energy security. The Government of Kenya holds majority ownership.

name: Kenya Power
description: Kenya Power and Lighting Company (KPLC) is the sole electricity distributor in Kenya, serving over six million metered connections for residential, commercial, and industrial customers. It purchases electricity from KenGen and independent power producers and recovers costs through regulated tariffs. The company faces chronic challenges from high system losses and the complexities of government-negotiated power purchase agreements.

name: Kenya Airways
description: Kenya Airways is Kenya's national carrier, operating international routes across Africa, Europe, and Asia as well as domestic and regional flights from its JKIA Nairobi hub. The airline has faced severe financial losses for over a decade and underwent a government-led balance sheet restructuring in 2021. Its recovery is closely tied to Africa's post-pandemic tourism rebound and the hub strategy for connecting East Africa to the world.

name: Kakuzi
description: Kakuzi is a diversified agricultural company with interests in avocado farming, tea growing, cattle ranching, macadamia nuts, and commercial forestry across Kenya. Its avocado exports to European supermarkets have been a key growth driver in recent years, capitalising on global avocado demand. Kakuzi is controlled by Camellia plc, a UK-headquartered agribusiness conglomerate.

name: Kurwitu Ventures
description: Kurwitu Ventures is a small investment holding company listed on the NSE. It has limited operational disclosures and is one of the least liquid stocks on the exchange. Its investment portfolio details are not widely covered by equity analysts.

name: Liberty Kenya Holdings
description: Liberty Kenya Holdings is a life insurance and asset management company, a subsidiary of Liberty Holdings of South Africa (itself a member of Standard Bank Group). It focuses on long-term life cover, annuities, and savings products for the Kenyan market. Liberty is one of the leading life insurance brands in Kenya.

name: Limuru Tea
description: Limuru Tea is a small tea estate company located in the Tigoni area of Kiambu County, in Kenya's Central Highlands. Its green leaf is processed at a nearby factory and sold at the Mombasa tea auction. The company is one of the lowest-liquidity stocks on the NSE with very thin daily trading volumes.

name: Longhorn Publishers
description: Longhorn Publishers is one of Kenya's leading educational content companies, producing textbooks and digital learning materials for primary, secondary, and tertiary education. It has expanded its distribution footprint across East and Central Africa. Revenue cycles are closely tied to government curriculum reviews, textbook procurement timelines, and school enrolment trends.

name: Nairobi Business Ventures
description: Nairobi Business Ventures is a small commercial services company listed on the NSE. It has limited analyst coverage and thin trading volumes, making it among the lower-liquidity names on the exchange. Its business activities are focused on the Kenyan domestic market.

name: NCBA Group
description: NCBA Group was created in 2019 through the merger of NIC Bank Group and Commercial Bank of Africa (CBA), making it one of Kenya's largest banks by assets. The group is renowned for pioneering mobile lending in Kenya through the M-Shwari product, developed in partnership with Safaricom, which has served millions of micro-borrowers. NCBA operates across Kenya, Uganda, Tanzania, Rwanda, and Ivory Coast.

name: Nation Media Group
description: Nation Media Group is the largest privately owned multimedia house in East Africa, operating the Daily Nation newspaper, Business Daily, NTV television, Easy FM radio, and a growing portfolio of digital media platforms. It operates in Kenya, Uganda, Tanzania, and Rwanda. The group is controlled by the Aga Khan Development Network and faces structural challenges from the global decline in print advertising.

name: Nairobi Securities Exchange
description: The Nairobi Securities Exchange (NSE) is itself a listed company, having demutualised and floated its own shares on its exchange in 2014. It operates Kenya's primary and secondary capital markets, including equities, bonds, and derivatives. NSE revenues come from listing fees, trading commissions, market data services, and technology licensing.

name: Olympia Capital Holdings
description: Olympia Capital Holdings is a Kenyan investment holding company with interests in commercial and display furniture manufacturing and commercial property. The company has had limited trading activity and is among the lower-liquidity stocks on the NSE. Its underlying investments serve the domestic commercial interiors market.

name: East African Portland Cement
description: East African Portland Cement is one of Kenya's oldest cement manufacturers but has faced persistent operational and financial difficulties for many years. The company is partly owned by the Government of Kenya and the National Social Security Fund (NSSF). Its market share has been significantly eroded by competition from newer, more efficient cement plants in Kenya.

name: Sasini Ltd
description: Sasini Ltd is an agricultural holding company with diversified interests in tea and coffee farming across Kenya's Central and Rift Valley regions. The company also has ancillary real estate and investment activities. It is one of the more actively traded agricultural stocks on the NSE and benefits from diversification across two major agricultural commodities.

name: Stanbic Holdings PLC
description: Stanbic Holdings is the Kenyan subsidiary of Standard Bank Group, one of Africa's largest banking and financial services groups headquartered in Johannesburg. It offers corporate, commercial, and retail banking services with particular strength in trade finance, corporate lending, and custody services. Stanbic is a key banker for multinational corporations and large Kenyan corporate groups.

name: WPP Scangroup
description: WPP Scangroup is the largest marketing and communications group in Sub-Saharan Africa, operating as a subsidiary of WPP plc of the UK. It provides advertising, public relations, media buying, digital marketing, and research services across East, West, and Southern Africa. Its revenues track the advertising spend cycles of the region's largest consumer goods and financial services companies.

name: Standard Chartered Bank Kenya
description: Standard Chartered Bank Kenya is a subsidiary of London-headquartered Standard Chartered plc, with operations in Kenya dating back to 1911. It focuses on upper-market retail banking, corporate banking, and financial markets for multinational and large domestic clients. The bank has a long track record in trade financing and cross-border treasury services within the East African region.

name: Safaricom
description: Safaricom is Kenya's dominant telecommunications company, holding the largest share of mobile voice, data, and mobile money subscribers in the country. It operates M-Pesa, Sub-Saharan Africa's leading mobile money platform, which has expanded into Ethiopia and other markets. Safaricom is the largest company on the NSE by market capitalisation and a bellwether for the Kenyan economy.

name: Standard Group
description: Standard Group is a Kenyan media conglomerate operating The Standard newspaper, KTN Home TV, KTN News, and Radio Maisha, among other print and digital properties. It is Kenya's second-largest listed media company by reach. The group faces structural pressure from declining print advertising revenues as digital media consumption grows.

name: Sanlam Allianz Holdings Kenya
description: Sanlam Allianz Holdings Kenya (formerly Sanlam Kenya) is a composite insurer formed from the partnership of Sanlam of South Africa and Allianz SE of Germany in East Africa. It offers general insurance, life insurance, and investment services to retail and corporate clients. The group benefits from the deep financial expertise and reinsurance capacity of its two international parent companies.

name: Sameer Africa
description: Sameer Africa is Kenya's only domestic tyre manufacturer, producing Yana-brand tyres, and also distributes international tyre and auto product brands. The company faces intense competition from cheaper imported Asian tyres and has reported persistent losses in recent years. Its operational challenges reflect the broader difficulty of competing in light manufacturing against low-cost imports.

name: Satrix MSCI World Feeder ETF
description: Satrix MSCI World Feeder ETF is a passively managed fund that tracks the MSCI World Index, providing NSE investors with exposure to hundreds of large-cap equities across developed markets globally. Managed by Satrix of South Africa, the ETF is priced in US dollars and offers geographical diversification beyond the East African equity market. It is designed for investors seeking global equity exposure through the NSE.

name: TotalEnergies EP Kenya
description: TotalEnergies EP Kenya, a subsidiary of the French energy major TotalEnergies, holds hydrocarbon exploration and production interests in Kenya, particularly in the Lokichar Basin oil discoveries in Turkana County. The project represents one of Sub-Saharan Africa's most significant onshore oil discoveries in recent decades. Development timelines have been subject to ongoing infrastructure and regulatory negotiations.

name: TPS Eastern Africa (Serena) Ltd
description: TPS Eastern Africa owns and manages the Serena Hotels and Lodges chain, comprising luxury hotels, boutique lodges, and safari camps across Kenya, Tanzania, Uganda, Rwanda, Mozambique, and Zanzibar. The company's revenues are directly tied to regional tourism volumes, foreign visitor arrivals, and conference tourism spending. It is partly owned by the Aga Khan Fund for Economic Development, which provides brand standards and strategic direction.

name: TRIFIC Green USD I-REIT
description: TRIFIC Green USD I-REIT is a US dollar-denominated Income REIT investing in green-certified commercial properties listed on the NSE. It distributes rental income in USD, offering investors both real estate income exposure and a partial currency hedge against the Kenya shilling. Green-certified properties target premium rental yields from ESG-focused corporate tenants.

name: Uchumi Supermarket Ltd
description: Uchumi Supermarket is one of Kenya's oldest retail chains but has been in severe financial distress for over a decade, struggling with accumulated debt, supply chain disruptions, and management turnover. The company has closed most of its branches and its recovery prospects remain highly uncertain. Its shares have experienced prolonged suspension periods on the NSE.

name: Umeme Ltd
description: Umeme Ltd is Uganda's largest electricity distribution company, operating under a 20-year concession granted by the Ugandan government. Cross-listed on both the Uganda Securities Exchange and the NSE, it distributes electricity to over 700,000 customers across Uganda. The concession model means Umeme's revenues are regulated and its financial returns are linked to capital reinvestment in Uganda's grid.

name: Unga Group
description: Unga Group is Kenya's leading grain miller, producing wheat flour, maize meal, and animal feed products under the Unga, Jogoo, Bakwell, and Promill brand names. It also operates a major animal feeds business serving the livestock industry across East Africa. The group's margins are highly sensitive to global grain prices, energy costs, and the strength of the Kenya shilling.

name: Williamson Tea Kenya
description: Williamson Tea Kenya is one of Kenya's largest tea estate companies, with properties in the Nandi Hills and Rift Valley regions. It produces black CTC tea for the Mombasa auction and for direct export, under ownership of Camellia plc of the UK. The company's performance is closely tied to global CTC black tea prices, seasonal rainfall in its growing zones, and the KES/USD exchange rate.

name: Express Kenya
description: Express Kenya is a logistics, freight forwarding, and warehousing company that capitalises on Kenya's role as a regional trade hub through the port of Mombasa. It provides customs clearance, cargo handling, and storage services to importers and exporters. The company is among the smaller, lower-liquidity stocks in the commercial services sector on the NSE.
